Conforming would require authoring a new reconciling clause and choosing where and how to place it, which is design, not derivation.

existing_claim: I attacked the two-controls seam for conformance to ADR-008 §2.2, testing whether DDR-003 collapses distillation-review and the EA promotion gate, or mis-maps either onto the wrong ADR-008 source class. It held: §1.2 correctly maps 'derive' to ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control (evidence-visible, per-distinct-lesson, distinctness-bounded — verbatim to ADR-008's row) and 'promote' to the promotion class's per-write EA gate, and correctly states a distillation approval 'makes a lesson consumable track-record and nothing more — Catalog/Standards entry still requires the EA gate,' preserving ADR-008's two-failure-mode calibration without conflation.
incoming_claim: Checked whether DDR-003 collapses the distillation-class review and the EA promotion gate into one control, or mis-maps either onto the wrong ADR-008 source class. It held: §1.2 maps 'derive' to ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control (substantive, evidence-visible, per-distinct-lesson, distinctness-bounded — verbatim to the ADR-008 row) and 'promote' to the promotion class's per-write EA gate (DDR-002 §2.4/§5, §7 #15), and correctly holds that a distillation approval 'makes a lesson consumable track-record and nothing more — Catalog/Standards entry still requires the EA gate.' The two-failure-mode calibration is preserved without conflation; the seam conforms to the cited authority.

existing_claim: §4.3 decides a binding coherence rule: 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data,' validated at configuration time. §7.1 class 3 decides 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role).' §3.1 decides signals — e.g. override-recurrence — that traverse multiple RG roles for a single detection (ReasoningProgress conclusions, their RejectedAlternative overrides, and per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM potentially Evidence). When one lookback reads a joined multi-role trail, the effective retention bound is the shortest window among the roles traversed, not any single role's window. The record decides both per-role windows and a per-window coherence rule but does not decide which role's window governs the multi-role read, leaving §4.3's binding rule underspecified for exactly the multi-role signals §3.1 commits.
incoming_claim: Decision.5 and §4.3 commit a binding coherence rule: 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data,' validated at configuration time. §7.1 class 3 decides 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role).' §3.1 decides signals — override-recurrence — that traverse a joined multi-role trail (ReasoningProgress conclusions, their RejectedAlternative overrides, and per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM potentially Evidence). When one lookback reads across roles carrying different windows, the effective retention floor is the shortest window among the roles traversed; §4.3 states the rule per-window against 'every detection lookback window that reads the retained data' but does not decide which role's window governs the cross-role read. The record decides both per-role windows and a per-window coherence rule without reconciling the multi-role read case §3.1 commits, leaving §4.3's binding validation undecided for exactly those signals.

existing_claim: Attacked the record for smuggled deferrals — a commitment made in body prose that rides along without surfacing as a decision or a named gap (a scope defect from this altitude regardless of merit). Traced each body deferral to §9: candidate-ObservedPattern shape (§5.1→§9), executed-retraction detection analogue (§6.2→§9), structured diagnosis capture (§2.2→§9), decision-consumed survival mechanism (§7.2→§9), multi-condition composition (§6.3→§9), all §4 calibration values (§4→§9), batch guardrails (§2.3→§9), access-model surface (§1.3→§9), retrieval affordance (§7.2→§9). Each has a matching §9 entry with owner and reversal path, and Pre-Acceptance Conditions correctly states every deferral is a named gap, not an acceptance condition. The deferral-declaration discipline held under attack.
incoming_claim: Attacked the record for smuggled deferrals — commitments made in body prose that ride along without being surfaced as a decision or a named gap (a scope defect from this altitude regardless of merit). Traced every body deferral to §9: candidate-ObservedPattern shape (§5.1→§9), executed-retraction detection analogue (§6.2→§9), structured diagnosis capture (§2.2→§9), decision-consumed survival mechanism (§7.2→§9), multi-condition composition (§6.3→§9), all §4 calibration values (§4→§9), batch guardrails (§2.3→§9), access-model surface (§1.3→§9), retrieval affordance (§7.2→§9). Each has a matching §9 entry with a named owner and a reversal path, and Pre-Acceptance Conditions correctly states every deferral is a named gap, not an acceptance condition. The deferral-declaration discipline held under attack.

existing_claim: Attacked the Decision-block-as-contract-index discipline: whether load-bearing rulings ride in the body without appearing in the enumeration. Traced the centerpiece rulings — two-controls seam (Decision.1/§1.2), five diagnosis dimensions (Decision.2/§2.2), role split with human-review amendment boundary (Decision.3/§1.3), no-leak invariant (Decision.4/§3.2), parameter mechanism (Decision.5/§4), distillation candidate/lifecycle/revalidation/archival (Decision.6/§5), remedy boundary (Decision.7/§6.1), conditional-scope (Decision.8/§6.3), cross-class four-outcome resolution (Decision.9/§6.4), retention-class model and audit posture (Decision.10/§7) — each traces to its body section and back. The load-bearing decisions all resolve in the enumeration; the index discipline held, with only under-surfaced components reported as defects above.
incoming_claim: Attacked the Decision-block-as-contract-index discipline — whether load-bearing rulings ride in the body without appearing in the enumeration. Traced the centerpiece rulings and back: two-controls seam (Decision.1/§1.2), five diagnosis dimensions (Decision.2/§2.2), role split with human-review amendment boundary (Decision.3/§1.3), no-leak invariant (Decision.4/§3.2), parameter mechanism (Decision.5/§4), distillation candidate/lifecycle/revalidation/archival (Decision.6/§5), remedy boundary (Decision.7/§6.1), conditional-scope (Decision.8/§6.3), cross-class four-outcome resolution (Decision.9/§6.4), retention-class model and audit posture (Decision.10/§7). Each traces to its body section and back. The load-bearing decisions all resolve in the enumeration; the index discipline held, with only under-surfaced components (the §6.1 classification axis, the §4.3 coherence rule) reported as defects.

existing_claim: §2.2 dimension 5 reads 'Conditional-vs-unconditional applicability — whether the knowledge is universally safe or scope-bounded; this dimension drives `approved_conditional` (§6.3).' §6.3 is titled 'Conditional-scope governance' and addresses the re-scope deliberation obligation and the multi-condition interim posture — the governance of *existing* conditional facts. It does not define how dimension-5 diagnosis produces an `approved_conditional` verdict or authors the initial `Condition` predicate at the gate; gate-time authoring appears only glancingly ('the predicate is EA-authored in the approval act'). A reader following the '(§6.3)' pointer for 'how dimension 5 drives approved_conditional' lands on re-scope/multi-condition content, not on the gate-time authoring the sentence promises. The cross-reference target is imprecise.
incoming_claim: §2.2 dimension 5 reads 'Conditional-vs-unconditional applicability — whether the knowledge is universally safe or scope-bounded; this dimension drives `approved_conditional` (§6.3).' §6.3 is titled 'Conditional-scope governance' and its decided content is the re-scope deliberation obligation and the multi-condition interim posture — the governance of existing conditional facts. It does not define how dimension-5 diagnosis produces an `approved_conditional` verdict or authors the initial `Condition` predicate at the gate; gate-time authoring appears only glancingly ('the predicate is EA-authored in the approval act'). A reader following the '(§6.3)' pointer for 'how dimension 5 drives approved_conditional' lands on re-scope/multi-condition content, not the gate-time authoring the sentence promises. The cross-reference resolves, but not to the content the reference asserts it carries.

existing_claim: DDR-003 commits that 'until that shape and the review surface land, no distillation write into the Operational plane is conforming' and enforces this only at 'ADR-008 §6's review checkpoint pending mechanization.' This is a hard-to-reverse posture that forecloses the Operational plane entirely for the platform's lifecycle until two forthcoming, unauthored designs land (the candidate shape via a future graph-schema amendment; the review surface via the detection-promotion service design). The record commits the platform to a position — the Operational plane is unwritable in a conforming way — whose reversibility depends on work not yet scheduled against any dependency. The justification offered ('no distillation process is built and no distillation traffic runs') establishes the cost is currently zero but does not establish the timing is proportionate: the record does not state whether the detection-promotion design is a near-term dependency or an open-ended forward item, leaving a lifecycle-spanning KG plane gated on unscoped work.
incoming_claim: §5.1 rules 'until that shape and the review surface land, no distillation write into the Operational plane is conforming,' enforced only at ADR-008 §6's review checkpoint pending mechanization in the detection-promotion service design (forthcoming). This forecloses a committed lifecycle-spanning KG plane — the Operational plane is unwritable in any conforming way — with reversibility resting on two forthcoming, unauthored designs (the candidate shape via a future graph-schema amendment; the review surface via the detection-promotion service design) whose dependency timing the record does not state. A hard-to-reverse foreclosure of a committed lifecycle stage gated on unscheduled work requires justification proportionate to the cost of undoing it; the record supplies 'no distillation traffic runs' (cost-currently-zero) but does not establish the timing is proportionate.

existing_claim: DDR-003 §6.2/§9 rules the executed-retraction detection analogue (the retraction-side mirror of the promoted-origin invariant) as deferred-with-trigger, justifying the deferral as safe because §6.2's forward-act-only rule makes the missing detection 'an audit-reconciliation concern rather than a consumption defect.' But ADR-008 §5.3 already named this exact gap as a standing *Risk* requiring tracking ('a re-decided executed retraction has no standing detection analogue yet ... the detection mechanics are a named schema / governance follow-up'). DDR-003, as the governance authority ADR-008 routes this to, accepts the deferral without re-weighing the ledger-reconciliation cost the upstream risk names — an unreconcilable governance ledger for the entire pre-first-retraction lifecycle. A deferral of an audit-integrity property that an upstream authority flagged as a risk deserves justification proportionate to that cost; 'non-load-bearing meanwhile' asserts the read-path is safe but does not address the audit-integrity dimension ADR-008 §5.3 raised.
incoming_claim: DDR-003 §6.2/§9 rules the executed-retraction detection analogue as deferred-with-trigger, justifying the deferral as safe because §6.2's forward-act-only rule makes the missing detection 'an audit-reconciliation concern rather than a consumption defect.' ADR-008 §5.3 already named this exact gap as a standing Risk requiring tracking ('a re-decided executed retraction has no standing detection analogue yet'). As the governance authority ADR-008 §2.6 routes this to, DDR-003 should stand as the record that re-weighs whether an unreconcilable governance ledger for the entire pre-first-retraction lifecycle is an acceptable posture; instead it accepts the deferral with 'non-load-bearing meanwhile,' addressing the read-path dimension while not addressing the audit-integrity dimension the upstream risk raised. Whether the platform should stand while carrying an un-detectable contested-retraction audit gap is a posture question deserving justification proportionate to the ledger-reconciliation cost.

existing_claim: Checked whether DDR-003 pulls against ADR-008's ratified principle layer by re-deciding what ADR-008 already fixed, or authors above its altitude. It held: the record consistently cites ADR-008 §2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.6 as upstream authority, authors only the policy layer ADR-008 §2.6 explicitly routes to the feedback-loop governance design (remedy boundary, diagnosis dimensions, roles, calibration, retention, audit), converts landed schema commitments (the gate invariants, the retraction shape, the supersession model) to citations rather than re-rulings, and states the one premise ADR-008 superseded (the formerly-ungated distillation write becoming the reviewed distillation class) as an applied learning rather than a silent re-decision. It does not re-litigate the entry-checkpoint principle or the promotion-class control-type; it instantiates them. The integration with the prior ratified decisions is sound at this altitude.
incoming_claim: Checked whether DDR-003 pulls against ADR-008's ratified principle layer by re-deciding what ADR-008 already fixed, or authors above its altitude. It held: the record consistently cites ADR-008 §2.2/§2.3/§2.4/§2.5/§2.6 as upstream authority, authors only the policy layer ADR-008 §2.6 explicitly routes to the feedback-loop governance design (remedy boundary, diagnosis dimensions, roles, calibration, retention, audit), converts landed schema commitments (the gate invariants, the retraction shape, the supersession model) to citations rather than re-rulings, and states the one premise ADR-008 superseded (the formerly-ungated distillation write becoming the reviewed distillation class) as an applied learning. It does not re-litigate the entry-checkpoint principle or the promotion-class control-type; it instantiates them. The integration with prior ratified decisions is sound at this altitude.

existing_claim: Attacked the remedy boundary for reversibility-proportionality — whether the retraction/supersession split commits a hard-to-reverse position without proportionate justification. It held: supersession is the default (reversible by lineage retention), retraction is the conjunctive narrow exception (should-never-have-been-promoted AND no correct replacement) carrying a durable why-no-replacement record, and re-admission is ruled as always a forward EA-gated act so no ledger side-effect can silently re-admit ground truth — un-retraction has a defined forward path via re-promotion. The irreversible-feeling act (retraction) is the narrow, well-documented exception, and its reversal path is explicit. The reversibility posture is proportionate to risk and correctly grounded in ADR-008 §2.6's replaceable-vs-retracted boundary.
incoming_claim: Attacked the retraction/supersession split for reversibility-proportionality — whether it commits a hard-to-reverse position without proportionate justification. It held at the boundary level: supersession is the default (reversible by lineage retention), retraction is the conjunctive narrow exception (should-never-have-been-promoted AND no correct replacement) carrying a durable why-no-replacement record, and re-admission is ruled as always a forward EA-gated act so no ledger side-effect can silently re-admit ground truth. The irreversible-feeling act is the narrow, well-documented exception with an explicit forward reversal path. The posture is proportionate to risk and grounded in ADR-008 §2.6's replaceable-vs-retracted boundary. (The soundness gap in the re-admission execution mechanism — 'via the supersession path' — is a distinct internal-correctness question outside this altitude.)

existing_claim: §4.3 binds 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data'; §7.1 class 3 carries 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role)'. §3.1's override-recurrence signal traverses multiple RG roles (ReasoningProgress conclusions and their RejectedAlternative overrides, and per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM potentially Evidence). When one lookback reads a joined multi-role trail it is bounded by the shortest window among the traversed roles, but §4.3's per-window rule does not resolve which role's window governs a cross-role read. The coherence rule is stated against 'every detection lookback window that reads the retained data' without reconciling the cross-role case, leaving it unenforceable as written for multi-role signals.
incoming_claim: §4.3 binds 'Every §7.1 working-tier retention window must be greater than or equal to every detection lookback window that reads the retained data', validated at configuration time. §7.1 class 3 carries 'per-role retention windows (one per RG role)'. §3.1's override-recurrence signal traverses a joined multi-role trail (ReasoningProgress conclusions and their RejectedAlternative overrides, and per DDR-002 §5 PROPOSED_FROM potentially Evidence). When one lookback reads across roles carrying different windows, the effective retention floor is the shortest window among the roles traversed, not any single role's window. §4.3's per-window rule does not resolve which role's window governs the cross-role read, leaving the coherence validation unspecified — and unenforceable as written — for exactly the multi-role signals §3.1 commits.

existing_claim: §5.2 states 'Resolving, superseding, or re-activating a distilled lesson is itself a distillation judgment under the same distillation-class review,' and §1.3 assigns lifecycle transitions to the distillation-reviewer role. ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control-type row is scoped to 'Substantive, evidence-visible human review of the distinct distilled judgment' — the *entry* of a distilled lesson, per-distinct-lesson. Withdrawal (resolve/supersede/archive) is governed by ADR-008 §2.4 and §2.1 scopes the invariant across a distinct 'withdrawal' lifecycle stage. DDR-003 extends the distillation *entry* control to cover *transitions* of an already-entered lesson without citing an ADR-008 locus that homes the Operational-plane withdrawal control on the distillation control.
incoming_claim: §5.2 states 'Resolving, superseding, or re-activating a distilled lesson is itself a distillation judgment under the same distillation-class review', and §1.3 assigns lifecycle transitions to the distillation-reviewer role, citing ADR-008 §2.4. ADR-008 §2.4's revalidation-obligation clause reads: 'its governed transition (a resolution or supersession, itself a distillation judgment under §2.2)' — which grants the resolve/supersede case. But §5.2 additionally homes RE-ACTIVATION (an admission-direction act, not a resolution or supersession) on the distillation review, and ADR-008 §2.4's granting clause names only 'a resolution or supersession'; re-activation of an archived lesson back into consumable track-record is an entry-adjacent act whose homing on the distillation-entry control §2.2 supplies no explicit locus for. The transition-control home is asserted for re-activation without a granting upstream locus.

existing_claim: DDR-003 §9 asserts its executed-retraction ruling is 'discharging the graph schema's §2.4 routing at the policy layer.' DDR-002 §2.4's retraction re-decision text routes two items to DDR-003: the un-retraction *remedy* and 'a standing detection analogue of #15 for executed retractions.' DDR-003 §6.2 rules the remedy (forward-act re-promotion) but explicitly re-defers the detection analogue to a named gap (§9). 'Discharging the routing at the policy layer' overstates: one of two routed items is discharged and the other re-deferred, so the routed obligation is partially met, not discharged.
incoming_claim: DDR-003 §9's executed-retraction-detection-analogue entry asserts 'the governance is ruled here, discharging the graph schema's §2.4 routing at the policy layer.' DDR-002 §2.4's retraction re-decision text routes two items to DDR-003: 'the un-retraction remedy — and a standing detection analogue of #15 for executed retractions ... — is not authored here.' §6.2 rules the remedy (forward-act re-promotion) but explicitly re-defers the detection analogue ('a named gap (§9)'). 'Discharging the routing at the policy layer' overstates against a routing that named two items: one is discharged and the other re-deferred, so the routed obligation is partially met, not discharged.

existing_claim: Decision.3 and §1.3 fix that a non-human role-holder is an ADR-008 §2.2 amendment question while role-holder widening is a routine access-model act. §3.4 permits detection as 'deterministic encoded logic over captured graph state' and §5.1 requires a distillation candidate excluded from traversal until the distillation-reviewer approves. The record does not declare whether the detection mechanism surfacing a candidate to the reviewer, and the candidate-review-surface automation, sit inside or outside the ADR-008 human-review boundary — the consequence of the human-only fence on the loop's automation surface is under-declared, leaving the assignment-vs-amendment line unfixed where automation of the review workflow (as distinct from the judgment) would fall.
incoming_claim: Decision.3 and §1.3 fix that a non-human role-holder is an ADR-008 §2.2 amendment question while role-holder widening is a routine access-model act. §3.4 permits detection as 'deterministic encoded logic over captured graph state' and §5.1 requires a distillation candidate excluded from traversal until the distillation-reviewer approves. The record does not declare whether the detection mechanism surfacing a candidate to the reviewer, and the candidate-review-surface automation, sit inside or outside the ADR-008 human-review boundary. ADR-008 §2.1 fixes that the control is on the judgment and the gateway (executor) is never the authoring authority; the consequence of that human-only fence on the loop's automation surface — where the assignment/amendment line falls when the review workflow (as distinct from the judgment) is automated — is under-declared.

existing_claim: §7.2 commits two interim survival exemptions (decision-consumed Evidence; promotion-consumed substrate) 'held until their deferred survival mechanism lands,' and §7.1 defers every class-3 window value to onboarding while §4.3 binds retention ≥ lookback at configuration time against those deferred values. The decision-consumed survival mechanism is itself an unscheduled §9 graph-schema gap. The record states the exemptions are 'reversible at zero traffic' but names no event that forces them to resolve into a real mechanism — so the interim is a standing commitment with no defined exit, and ADR-008 §2.4's decision-rationale-survival guarantee rests on an open-ended exemption rather than a bounded bridge.
incoming_claim: §7.2 commits two interim survival exemptions ('Interim: class-2 Evidence is exempt from expiry until a survival mechanism for decision-consumed Evidence lands' and the promotion-consumed-substrate retention exemption) 'held until their deferred survival mechanism lands.' The decision-consumed survival mechanism is itself a named-but-unscheduled §9 graph-schema gap ('a graph-schema question, deferred by name'). The record states the exemptions are reversible 'at zero traffic' but names no event that forces them to resolve into a real mechanism — the interim is therefore a standing commitment with no defined exit rather than a bounded bridge, and ADR-008 §2.4's decision-rationale-survival guarantee rests on an open-ended exemption whose accumulating never-expiring retention (once traffic begins) grows with every gated promotion.

existing_claim: Attacked the two-controls seam for collapse or mis-mapping onto the wrong ADR-008 source class. It held: §1.2 maps 'derive' to ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control (evidence-visible, per-distinct-lesson, distinctness-bounded — verbatim to ADR-008's row) and 'promote' to the promotion class's per-write EA gate, and states a distillation approval 'makes a lesson consumable track-record and nothing more — Catalog/Standards entry still requires the EA gate.' The two failure modes stay distinct; neither control is conflated with the other, and the entry-vs-promote authority boundary is preserved. Checked against ADR-008 §2.2's calibration table.
incoming_claim: Checked whether DDR-003 collapses the distillation-class review and the EA promotion gate into one control, or mis-maps either onto the wrong ADR-008 source class. It held: §1.2 maps 'derive' to ADR-008 §2.2's operational-distillation control (substantive, evidence-visible, per-distinct-lesson, distinctness-bounded — verbatim to the ADR-008 row) and 'promote' to the promotion class's per-write EA gate (DDR-002 §2.4/§5, §7 #15), and states a distillation approval 'makes a lesson consumable track-record and nothing more — Catalog/Standards entry still requires the EA gate.' The two failure modes stay distinct; neither control is conflated with the other, and the entry-vs-promote authority boundary is preserved against the cited calibration table.

reason: D4/D7 restate Decision.10 as three RG classes + Operational posture and fix class 3 as per-role windows; neither ruling decides where non-selected candidates' Evidence lands. The finding's own coherence claim is that class-3's 'outside classes 1–2' catch-all conflicts with §7.1's explicit deferral of that same case to §9 — resolving it requires deciding whether non-selected Evidence falls into class 3 or the gap, a choice no named authority makes.
